title: Daily Market Digest: Major Indices Enter Correction Amid Geopolitical Tensions
subtitle: March 28, 2026 Market Close

items.headline: Stocks Tumble as Dow Confirms Correction Territory, Fifth Straight Weekly Decline for Major Indices
items.summary: U.S. equities experienced a significant downturn on Friday, March 28, 2026, marking the fifth consecutive week of losses, the longest streak in nearly four years. Escalating Middle East tensions, surging oil prices, and persistent inflation concerns fueled a broad market sell-off, pushing the Dow Jones Industrial Average into correction territory. Consumer sentiment also eased to a three-month low, reflecting growing economic uncertainty.

items.statContext: Up 3.61 points, highest close since April 21

items.headline: Energy Sector Outperforms Amid Surging Oil Prices
items.summary: Exxon Mobil ($XOM) saw gains as U.S. crude prices climbed 5.46% to settle at $99.64 a barrel, driven by geopolitical tensions.

items.headline: Amazon Drags on S&P 500
items.summary: Megacap technology stocks were a significant drag on the market, with Amazon ($AMZN) experiencing a notable decline.

items.headline: Consumer Discretionary Sector Weakness
items.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings ($NCLH) was among the companies selling non-essential goods and services that sank sharply as consumer sentiment weakened.

items.headline: Meta Platforms Experiences Steep Decline
items.summary: Meta Platforms ($META) fell significantly as the technology sector faced intense selling pressure.

items.headline: Middle East Tensions Drive Oil Prices Higher, Fuel Inflation Fears
items.summary: Escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, particularly the ongoing conflict involving Iran and concerns over the Strait of Hormuz, continue to exert significant pressure on global financial markets. Oil prices surged, with U.S. crude settling up 5.46% at $99.64 a barrel, intensifying inflation anxieties and contributing to broad-based equity losses.

items.headline: Fed Maintains Rates, Rate Cut Hopes Fade Amid Inflation Concerns
items.summary: The Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged at its March 18 meeting. With inflation remaining above the Fed's target and surging energy prices, markets are now factoring in no further rate cuts for 2026, with some pricing in a modest chance of a rate hike.

items.headline: Energy Leads, Tech and Consumer Discretionary Lag Amid Market Downturn
items.summary: For the week ending March 27, the energy sector was the best-performing, gaining 6.12%. In contrast, communication services and technology were the worst-performing sectors, falling 4.46% and 3.51% respectively, as megacap tech stocks faced selling pressure.

items.headline: U.S. Consumer Sentiment Eases to Three-Month Low in March
items.summary: U.S. consumer sentiment data for March revealed a decline to a three-month low, raising concerns about the broader economic outlook. This weakened sentiment contributed to the market's cautious tone.
